title: [InstCombine] Drop exact flag instead of increasing demanded bits
summary:
The generalization strategy identifies that when `SimplifyDemandedBits` modifies an exact shift's operand, the exactness guarantee may no longer hold. The original code conservatively preserved exactness by demanding low bits, but this blocked optimizations where those bits weren't actually needed for the final result.

The patch removes the special-case demand for low bits on exact shifts. Instead, it allows full simplification of the operand based on the post-shift demanded mask. If simplification occurs, it proactively drops poison-generating flags (including `exact`) on the shift instruction, since the operand's low bits may have changed.

This enables more aggressive dead-code elimination and constant folding, as shown in the generalized test cases where exact shifts of operations like `add`, `select`, or `umax` can now be optimized when their low bits are not demanded by later uses. The key insight is to separate the concern: optimize freely first, then clean up flags that may have become invalid.
